qt: A well designed Internal Control Questionnaire (ICQ) should incorporate the following EXCEPT:
op: Be organized by individual functional organizations
Should include simple yes or no responses but not narrative responses
Be a sufficient source of data for the evaluation of internal controls
Identify the internal control system’s strengths and weaknesses
ca: Be a sufficient source of data for the evaluation of internal controls

qt: Substantive procedures are tests which are undertaken to obtain which one of the followings?
Tests of controls are used to obtain evidence regarding the:
Tests of controls include all of the following EXCEPT:
Highlight the statements which truly described the method of re-performance by auditor EXCEPT;
op: To verify control over cash receipt

qt: Existence of purchasing department entertain the auditor in following manner:
op: Give awareness whether ordering is centralized or not
Give awareness to auditor about nature of goods purchased
Give awareness about entity’s minimum and maximum re-order level to ensure efficient control

qt: Inappropriate audit approaches lead towards misunderstanding of internal control systems. The best example for an inappropriate audit approach is;
op: Apply tests of control only for a particular assertion
Apply substantive procedures only for a particular assertion may be because auditor failed to identify any effective control
Apply the tests of controls only after occurring of event
Combined approach (applying both tests of operating effectiveness of control’s and substantive procedures for same assertion
ca: Apply tests of control only for a particular assertion

qt: Information that an Auditor may use as an audit evidence consists of the following EXCEPT:
Audit evidence in the form of facts which do not need any further guesswork or reasoning is called:
Types of analytical procedures may include the following EXCEPT:
Which one of the following primary assertions is satisfied when an auditor observes the client's physical count of inventory?
Following are included in the analytical procedure EXCEPT:
op: Comparison of information for the period examined with the previous period
Comparison of the examined information with its budget
Examine the relationship between financial and non-financial data

qt: Techniques of Audit Tests include all of the following EXCEPT:
From the following options select independent variable which has NO relation to substantive procedures;
If an auditor perform a process such as “compares the production cost with previous month’s cost” than this process is called as;
Reliability of audit evidence signifies the following implications EXCEPT:
op: Photocopied and facsimile documentation could helpful
Effectiveness of internal control should be evident
ca: Photocopied and facsimile documentation could helpful

qt: Test of details is also called as:
Highlight the factor which has NO effects on quality of Confirmations made by an auditor if he ignores it:
Which one of the following examples constitutes the most reliable piece of audit evidence available to an auditor?
op: A supplier’s invoice kept in the client's records
A written confirmation from a bank
A duplicate sales receipt dated and signed
ca: A written confirmation from a bank

qt: For ensuring the quality of Internal Control Questionnaire (ICQ), Auditor includes some reference in this respect. Highlight the reference which is NOT suitable to be included in ICQ;
Which one of the following elements is NOT related to audit quality?
Following are some main assertions used in substantive procedures EXCEPT:
While designing the substantive analytical procedures auditor should considers following matters EXCEPT:
Following are the basic objectives of constructing the Internal control questionnaire (ICQ) EXCEPT:
op: To identify those controls which indicate the strengths of system
To ensures about the proper dispatching of engagement letter
To identify those controls which indicate the weakness of system
To ensures about evaluation of control system thus recorded
ca: To ensures about the proper dispatching of engagement letter

qt: Analytical procedures are used to understand the:
Internal sources of audit evidence for an entity includes the following EXCEPT:
Audit evidence, including information received from the audited entity in writing or oral is called:
External source of obtaining audit evidence are listed below EXCEPT:
Sound internal control helped out the auditor to entertain some relaxation, such as:
op: Helped the auditor to express his opinion at the end of audit process
Reduces the volume of transactions testing required in conducting an audit
Helped the auditor to check available records
ca: Reduces the volume of transactions testing required in conducting an audit

qt: Appropriateness refers to the quality of audit evidence. For evidence to be appropriate, it should integrate which one of the followings?
op: Received only from a third party
Documented in the audit working papers

qt: SOFs are special types of forms used in internal control questionnaire (ICQ) process. These form basically named as:
Which of the following is an integral part of audit at the stage of checking and evaluation of information?
Test of controls comprise of testing all of the following three things EXCEPT:
Documentary evidence is used extensively in auditing. Which one of the following represents two special classes of documentary evidence?
During tests of control procedure its design can easily tested with the help of:
Which one of the following is FALSE about Internal Control Evaluation Checklists (ICEC)?
op: It is normally employed where system’s information has already been recorded
ICEC is developed to overcome the shortcomings of Internal Control Questionnaire
The rules of constructing ICEC is different than construction of Internal Control Questionnaire
It is designed to determine; whether desirable internal controls are present
ca: The rules of constructing ICEC is different than construction of Internal Control Questionnaire

qt: Which one of the following audit procedures is most associated with the management’s assertion of ‘Completeness’?
op: Searching for all unmatched receiving reports
Checking that the accounts receivable aged trial balance adds up
Confirming an accounts receivable balance with a customer
ca: Searching for all unmatched receiving reports

qt: Which one of the following is an alternative name for ‘Test of controls’?
Auditor is able to apply tests of control procedure if following condition are fulfilled EXCEPT:
op: Only applied after application of test of details procedure
When sound internal control exists in the organization
When sufficient appropriate audit evidence can’t be obtained at assertion level by substantive procedures alone
ca: Only applied after application of test of details procedure

qt:
The Nature of audit procedures refers to which of the following:

I.	Its Purpose
II.	Its Type
III.	Its Duration
IV.	Its features
Audit quality refers to the following statements EXCEPT:
op: Discharging responsibilities with due professional care

qt: Which of the following is TRUE for audit evidence?
op: It is the information to form an opinion on marketing statements
It is the information to form an opinion on financial statements
It is the information to form an opinion on management statements
It is the information to form an opinion on audit statements
ca: It is the information to form an opinion on financial statements

qt: ‘Timing of audit procedure’ refers to which one of the following descriptions?
op: It refers to sample size or number of observations of a control activity (quantity of audit evidence)
It refers to the nature of testing and their procedures, that is, designing, structuring, and compiling work to be done
It refers to when audit procedures are performed or the period or date to which the audit evidence applies

It refers to the purpose i.e. (tests of controls or substantive procedures) and their type, that is, inspections, observation, inquiry confirmation, recalculation, re-performances or analytical procedures
ca: It refers to when audit procedures are performed or the period or date to which the audit evidence applies

qt: The quantity of audit evidence needed is NOT effected by:
Certain assertions were used while obtaining the audit evidence. “Cutoff” is an important type of assertion used in above mention process, which statement best described the “cutoff” assertion?
op: Transactions and events have been recorded in proper period
Appropriate amount and data have been recorded
Transactions and events have been recorded in proper accounts
ca: Transactions and events have been recorded in proper period

qt: Test of control is a brief method to obtain the audit evidence. It comprises of three things such as:
op: Design, implement & Checked the Operating efficiency
Design, implement & Checked the Operating effectiveness
ca: Design, implement & Checked the Operating effectiveness

qt: The process of studying the legitimacy, usefulness and reliability of business transactions is:
op: Examination of violations found in operations

qt: Appropriateness of the audit evidence refers to the quality of audit evidence that imply to which one of the following statements?
op: Relevance and reliability of audit evidence gathered
Inconsistency and flexibility of audit evidence gathered
Formulation and structure of audit evidence gathered
Consequence and implication of audit evidence gathered
ca: Relevance and reliability of audit evidence gathered

qt: Audit evidence, including information received from third parties in writing is called:
An auditor of a large telecom company gathers audit evidence to determine if specific management assertions are being met. He should perform the specific facts  as follows EXCEPT:
While inspecting the purchase cycle of a company the auditor focuses to obtain the following objectives EXCEPT:
op: Goods received should be properly inspected
Purchase order issued under proper authority and procedures
Purchase orders only issues for such quantity which is necessary for the conducting of business operations
All transaction related to receivables should be properly recorded
ca: All transaction related to receivables should be properly recorded

qt: For better results, Auditor focused on designing the question while ICQ constructions. Questions should be designed to fulfill the following objectives EXCEPT:
op: It ensures the management participation in ICQ constructions
It ensure whether the control objectives are being achieved or not
It ensures that the all aspects were covered
It ensures about the validity of information obtaining in response
ca: It ensures the management participation in ICQ constructions

qt: If we talk about timings of substantive procedures. Which statement seems to be true?
op: Substantive procedure are more reliable if applied at the end of year
Substantive procedure are more reliable if applied frequently during the year
Substantive procedure are more reliable if applied after specific intervals in a year at least 2 times
Substantive procedure are more reliable if applied at start of the year
ca: Substantive procedure are more reliable if applied at the end of year

qt:
For better results an ICQ should carry basic information as:

I.   Yes and No answer
II.  Comments with Yes and No answer are not applicable
III. Consists of open ended questions only
IV.  All respondents should properly literate
Inspection of tangible assets is a good example of the following assertion for obtaining audit evidence:

op: Source and nature of the information

qt: Any cancellation of sale/purchase invoice leads toward further cancellation of which one of the following:
Inventory should be identified and marked with accordance to relevant information, this information can be related to;
Which of  the following matters should be included in the letter of representation?
op: Market value of the company’s stock
Existence of any fraud or error in the statements
Company’s growth trend in the future in figures
Existence of any immaterial mistake in the statements
ca: Existence of any fraud or error in the statements

qt: Which one of the following testing objectives refers to the assertion “Completeness” in obtaining audit evidence?
op: Transactions arising during the period are reflected in the period's financial statements
Assets and liabilities included on the balance sheet actually exist
The amounts at which assets and liabilities are stated is correct
All transactions and events that should be recorded have been recorded
ca: All transactions and events that should be recorded have been recorded

qt: Which one of the following documents is NOT included in purchase system while applying different control procedures on it?
In order to ensure effective control, how often a full inventory count should be held when perpetual inventory records are not kept adequately?
When payroll costs are compared with the number of employees, the auditor would be able to:
op: Obtain evidence of management’s control over the preparation of payroll
Point out over employment in the entity
Detect an overpayment to one of the employees
See that payroll costs are reasonable
ca: See that payroll costs are reasonable

qt: Following are some Non-Statutory payroll deductions made by the organization from worker’s pay EXCEPT:
op: Fee paid for subscription to trade union

qt: For measuring the quality of audit evidence auditor used the tools of appropriateness; in case if auditor wants to measure quantity of audit evidence which tools from the following should be adopted?
Receivable ledger control accounts should be prepared regularly and should checked by the following authority:
According to the requirements of auditing standards for Wages and Salaries, the authorization to employ or dismiss any employee should be done in which one of the following ways?
Which one of the following is NOT a general principle with respect to verification method of liabilities?
op: To verify all liabilities have been stated
To determine terms and conditions agreed when accepting loans
To consider the reasonableness of the liability
To consider the liabilities at previous accounting date
ca: To consider the liabilities at previous accounting date

qt: Adjustments should be made before ending the financial period. These adjustments regarding purchase system should be recorded in:
According to the requirements of auditing standards for Wages and Salaries, who should be allowed to take wages of another employee in a company?
How often the physical inspection of non current assets should be carried out?
Which statement from the following is true for describing the Sinking Funds?
op: That type of funds which are kept aside by the organization to meet the needs of daily routine works
That type of funds which are kept aside by the organization to meet the normal loss
That type of funds which are kept aside by the organization to meet the debts
That type of funds which are kept aside by the organization to meet the Abnormal loss
ca: That type of funds which are kept aside by the organization to meet the debts

qt: The term ‘Reconciliation’ refers to which one of the following statements in the context of auditing?
op: A special Congressional procedure often used to implement the revenue and spending targets established in the budget resolution
Reconciliation refers to an audit test that compares three sets of financial records to make sure they are in agreement
Reconciliation refers to a process that of preparing final audit opinion
Reconciliation refers to a process that compares two sets of financial records to make sure they are in agreement
ca: Reconciliation refers to a process that compares two sets of financial records to make sure they are in agreement

Purchase system in an organization starts from purchase order and it ends up with;

op: Cheques, cash and postal orders received
ca: Cheques, cash and postal orders received

qt: Control procedures are developed mainly upon which one of the following basis?
Which one of the following is an example of designing control procedure for achieving control objectives?
op: List all the invoices in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of details
List all the documents in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of controls
List all the orders in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of skills
List all the returns in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate evidence for their existence
ca: List all the documents in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of controls

qt: Following aspects of assets must be verified EXCEPT:

op: List all the orders in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of skills
List all the invoices in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of details
List all the returns in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate evidence for their existence
List all the documents in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of controls
ca: List all the documents in a transaction cycle & generate appropriate test of controls

qt: What the auditor should perceive about a situation when management refuses to provide necessary representations?
op: For auditor, it constitutes a scope limitation
The auditor should try to obtain alternative audit evidence
The auditor should resign from the management
For auditor, this situation could lead to give qualified opinion
ca: For auditor, it constitutes a scope limitation

qt: Which of the following element is NOT considerd while preparing payslip of any worker?

Compensating balance of cash can be easily described with the help of following statement;
op: Amount that is used to maintain service charges by bank from deposit
Amount that is required for opening a new account in the bank
Amount that must be left on deposit and cannot be withdrawn
ca: Amount that must be left on deposit and cannot be withdrawn

qt: Which one of the following is an appropriate evidence for verifying the existence of inventory?
op: Obtaining a letter of representation from the management of the entity about it

qt: Law says that the general ledger account of petty cash will remain dormant at a set amount. It means that the custodian should maintain the petty cash balance equal to opening balance. This theory is named as;
“Grid stamp” is a term which is used to describe that:
op: Several signature by different authorities present on the face of document
Specific authority has checked the accuracy of document itself
Nominee of specific authority checked the accuracy of document
ca: Several signature by different authorities present on the face of document

qt: Following are usual methods of recording workers attendance EXCEPT:
Which one of the following best describes the auditors’ aim of testing a sales system of a company?
op: It is aimed at providing guidance for sales strategies and its management
It is aimed at providing effective guidance for conducting the audit of Sales
It is aimed at providing guidance for meeting the sales target
It is aimed at providing guidance for conducting the sales audit according to the management view of the company
ca: It is aimed at providing effective guidance for conducting the audit of Sales

qt: An input VAT tax should be recorded in the following manner
op: Recorded separately from the expense element of invoice total
ca: Recorded separately from the expense element of invoice total

qt: While testing the purchase system of an entity, which one of the following control tests may be applied to the Credit Notes by the auditors?
op: Evidence of approval of credit notes
Evidence of matching credit notes to goods returned notes
ca: Evidence of matching credit notes to goods returned notes

qt: Which one of the following is TRUE about Auditing requirements for transparency of the sales system of a company?
op: The Copies of cancelled invoices should be retained by the companies
The Copies of cancelled invoices should be kept in electronic records
The Copies of cancelled invoices should be given to the suppliers also
The Copies of cancelled invoices is not required to be retained by the companies
ca: The Copies of cancelled invoices should be retained by the companies

qt: Purchase invoices should be matched with which one of the followings?
op: Goods received notes and company orders
ca: Goods received notes and company orders

qt: How unused cheques should be handled in order to exercise effective internal control?
op: Should be sent to the central bank
Should be held in a secure place
Should be sent to the clearing agency
ca: Should be held in a secure place

qt: Which one from the following is NOT included in the category of cash?

Test of controls over inventory systems may include the following EXCEPT:
op: Assess adequacy of inventory counting procedures and attend count to ensure they are carried out
Check sequences of dispatch and goods received notes for completeness
Observe physical security of inventories and environment in which they are held
Count physical inventory daily and check inventory stock outs
ca: Count physical inventory daily and check inventory stock outs

qt: Employer total cost for wages and salaries consists of:
op: Total wages & Total salaries plus Employer’s provident contribution
Gross wages & Gross salaries plus Employer’s provident contribution
ca: Gross wages & Gross salaries plus Employer’s provident contribution

qt: Those items which are easily acceptable to a bank for deposits and are free from restrictions is(are):
When should cash payments receipts of each day be recorded?
op: Should be recorded next day in the cash book
Should be recorded monthly in the cash book
Should be recorded promptly in the cash book
Should not be recorded in the cash book
ca: Should be recorded promptly in the cash book

To confirm the movements in reserves are properly authorized

ca: Seek evidence of such cost which is ordinarily available

op: It refers to the tax which a company would charge on items which it sells
It refers to the tax which the company is charged when it sells its products to the retailers
It refers to the tax which the company is charged when it makes its purchases
It refers to the tax which a company would charge on items which it sells internationally
ca: It refers to the tax which the company is charged when it makes its purchases

qt: What is the treatment of social security benefit received by a worker of an organization?
op: Subtract it from bonus he received
Add it to total pay received
Add it to total income received
ca: Add it to total pay received

It depends upon the judgmental skills of the auditor

ca: It depends upon the judgmental skills of the auditor

The duties of the wages staff should preferably be rotated according to their performance
The duties of the wages staff should preferably be rotated monthly

ca: The duties of the wages staff should preferably be rotated during the year

ca: Details of each item, its cost and accumulated depreciation

ca: It is the information to form an opinion on financial statements

qt: Substantive procedures are tests which are undertaken to obtain which one of the followings?
Documentary evidence is used extensively in auditing. Which one of the following represents two special classes of documentary evidence?
For better results, Auditor focused on designing the question while ICQ constructions. Questions should be designed to fulfill the following objectives EXCEPT:
op: It ensures about the validity of information obtaining in response
It ensure whether the control objectives are being achieved or not
It ensures the management participation in ICQ constructions
It ensures that the all aspects were covered
ca: It ensures the management participation in ICQ constructions

qt: Test of controls comprise of testing all of the following three things EXCEPT:
Auditor is able to apply tests of control procedure if following condition are fulfilled EXCEPT:
op: When sufficient appropriate audit evidence can’t be obtained at assertion level by substantive procedures alone
When sound internal control exists in the organization
Only applied after application of test of details procedure
ca: Only applied after application of test of details procedure

qt: Highlight the factor which has NO effects on quality of Confirmations made by an auditor if he ignores it:
Which one of the following primary assertions is satisfied when an auditor observes the client's physical count of inventory?
Test of control is a brief method to obtain the audit evidence. It comprises of three things such as:
op: Design, implement & Checked the Operating efficiency
Design, implement & Checked the Operating effectiveness
ca: Design, implement & Checked the Operating effectiveness

qt: Which one of the following audit procedures is most associated with the management’s assertion of ‘Completeness’?
op: Checking that the accounts receivable aged trial balance adds up
Searching for all unmatched receiving reports
Confirming an accounts receivable balance with a customer
ca: Searching for all unmatched receiving reports

qt: Audit quality refers to the following statements EXCEPT:
op: Discharging responsibilities with due professional care

qt: Certain assertions were used while obtaining the audit evidence. “Cutoff” is an important type of assertion used in above mention process, which statement best described the “cutoff” assertion?
op: Transactions and events have been recorded in proper period
Transactions and events have been recorded in proper accounts
Appropriate amount and data have been recorded
ca: Transactions and events have been recorded in proper period

qt: SOFs are special types of forms used in internal control questionnaire (ICQ) process. These form basically named as:

For better results an ICQ should carry basic information as:

I.   Yes and No answer
II.  Comments with Yes and No answer are not applicable
III. Consists of open ended questions only
IV.  All respondents should properly literate
Audit evidence, including information received from the audited entity in writing or oral is called:
Sound internal control helped out the auditor to entertain some relaxation, such as:
op: Helped the auditor to check available records
Reduces the volume of transactions testing required in conducting an audit
Helped the auditor to express his opinion at the end of audit process
ca: Reduces the volume of transactions testing required in conducting an audit

qt: Which one of the following is an alternative name for ‘Test of controls’?
For ensuring the quality of Internal Control Questionnaire (ICQ), Auditor includes some reference in this respect. Highlight the reference which is NOT suitable to be included in ICQ;
Verification of debtors’ balances includes which of the following?
op: Verify debts with reference to cash received since year-end
Verify nature, amount and classification of credit balance
Check accuracy and completeness of debtors' listing

qt: Which of the following points may NOT be considered during verification of debtors’ balances?
op: Check accuracy and completeness of debtors' listing
Verify nature, amount and classification of credit balance
Check credit note cut-off, if material

qt: A building company constructs its own office block. How the company should identify this asset in the costing records?
op: Record the asset with intangible fixed assets
Record the asset separately in costing records
Record the asset in separately in liabilities
Record the asset in capital investments
ca: Record the asset separately in costing records

qt: Letter of confirmation may include the following information EXCEPT:
op: Full title of account and balances thereon

qt: Which one of the following best describes the purpose of letter of confirmation from the bank?
op: To check the bank balances in order to finally give true and fair view
To check the bank balances in order to ensure the financial health of the company
To confirm the bank balances and other matters before the audit process start in a company
To confirm the bank balances and other matters to the auditor directly
ca: To confirm the bank balances and other matters to the auditor directly
